name: Chocolate-Coconut Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat shortening and butter until softened.
2. Add the 3/4 cup sugar and beat until fluffy.
3. Add milk, egg, and 1 tsp of vanilla, then mix well.
4. In a separate bowl, combine flour, cocoa, baking powder, and salt.
5. Add this mixture to the butter mixture and beat until well mixed.
6. Divide dough into two halves and chill for 2-3 hours or until easy to handle.
7. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
8. Grease cookie sheets.
9. Mix softened cream cheese, 1/3 cup sugar, and 1 tsp vanilla until smooth.
10. Stir in the coconut.
11. Roll each half of the dough into a 10-inch square.
12. Cut each square into sixteen 2 1/2-inch squares.
13. Place cookies 1/2 inch apart on an ungreased cookie sheet.
14. Cut 1-inch slits from each corner to the center of each square.
15. Drop a level teaspoon of the coconut mixture onto each center.
16. Sprinkle chopped nuts in the center and firmly press to seal.
17. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until edges are firm and cookies are slightly puffed.
18. Cool on cookie sheet for 1 minute, then remove and cool thoroughly on a rack.
19. Makes 32 cookies
